topic.what: Analyzes the 2025 Signalgate leak where encrypted Signal messages containing US military operations were published by a journalist inadvertently added to a government chat group
topic.how: Uses pi-calculus formal modeling to prove the SCIF setup couldn't prevent leaks, then examines socio-political factors including power imbalances, false security sense, and governance failures
topic.why_matters: Demonstrates that usable encryption does not equal secure communications for average users; highlights gap between mathematical and socio-political security with geopolitical consequences

characteristics.what_it_measures: Difference between mathematical encryption capability and actual message confidentiality
characteristics.vs_baseline: Mathematical security achieved but socio-political security failed
characteristics.evidence: Signal encrypts correctly but messages appeared on NYT front page
characteristics.context: 30 years after Why Johnny Can't Encrypt, JD still cannot encrypt

open_problems: How to make average users achieve socio-political security
How to constrain actors with authority over security operators
How to prevent false security sense from causing oversharing
Bridging gap between encryption usability and actual secure deployment
